title: Autonomous AI Systems

modules.title: Self-Driving Vehicle Technology
modules.sub: How perception, planning, and control enable autonomous driving
modules.description: Learners examine the core technology stack behind self-driving vehicles, including sensor fusion, computer vision, and path planning algorithms. They analyze how these systems make real-time decisions and assess the maturity of current commercial deployments.

modules.title: Autonomous Drones and Robots
modules.sub: Extending autonomy beyond roads to air and physical tasks
modules.description: Learners explore how autonomy principles transfer to drones and robotic systems operating in warehouses, agriculture, and hazardous environments. They evaluate the distinct technical and regulatory challenges each platform presents and compare deployment strategies across industries.

modules.title: Safety and Reliability
modules.sub: Designing autonomous systems that fail gracefully and safely
modules.description: Learners investigate the engineering approaches used to ensure safety and reliability in autonomous systems, including redundancy, fault detection, and formal verification. They apply risk assessment frameworks to identify failure modes and evaluate how organizations validate systems before deployment.

modules.title: Human Oversight Requirements
modules.sub: Defining the right balance between automation and human control
modules.description: Learners examine regulatory standards, liability frameworks, and operational protocols that govern human oversight of autonomous systems. They make recommendations about appropriate levels of human intervention for different risk contexts and assess how oversight requirements are evolving globally.

synopsis: Explore AI systems that operate independently in the physical world. Understand the technology behind autonomous vehicles, drones, and robots.
